title: AN ACT STANDARDIZING THE SALARIES OF THE PROSECUTION STAFF OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, PROVINCIAL

chunks.text:
H. No. 3700 / 68 OG No. 9, 1701 (February 28, 1972)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6407, October 04, 1971 ]

AN ACT STANDARDIZING THE SALARIES OF THE PROSECUTION STAFF OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, PROVINCIAL

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. Section two of Republic Act Numbered Three thousand seven hundred eighty-three, as amended, is further amended to read as follows:

"SEC. 2. The Chief State Prosecutor, the Assistant Chief State Prosecutor, the Senior State Prosecutors, the State Prosecutors, and the District State Prosecutors shall be appointed by the President of the Philippines with the consent of the Commission on Appointments:

Provided

, That each judicial district as organized under the provisions of Republic Act Numbered Two hundred ninety-six, otherwise known as the Judiciary Act of 1948, as amended, shall have one District State Prosecutor who shall be permanently appointed thereto:

Provided, further,

That the District State Prosecutors

shall be under the supervision of the Chief State Prosecutor.

"The salary of the Chief State Prosecutor shall be P23,000.00 per annum and for each Assistant Chief State Prosecutor P22,000.00

per annum.

The qualifications and rank of the Senior State Prosecutor, State Prosecutor or District State Prosecutor shall be the same as a provincial fiscal."

SEC. 3. Section one of Republic Act Numbered Forty-one hundred forty-two is hereby amended to read as follows:

"SECTION 1. The annual salaries of provincial and city fiscals shall be as hereinbelow fixed:

"(A) For first class province or City"

SEC. 4. In addition to such sum as may have been provided in the current Appropriations Act, there is hereby appropriated, out of any funds in the National Treasury not otherwise appropriated, such sums as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act.

SEC. 5. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved, October 4, 1971.
